name: Illinois Apple Pie
recipeIngredient: 6 cups tart apples, peeled and sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F.
2. For the crust: In a mixing bowl, combine flour and salt. Cut in shortening with a pastry blender or two knives until the mixture is uniform. Gradually add water, tossing lightly with a fork until dough forms a ball. Divide dough into two parts.
3. On a lightly floured surface, roll out the bottom crust to form a circle about 1/8 inch thick and approximately 1 1/2 inches larger than an inverted 9-inch pie plate. Gently ease the dough into the pie plate without stretching it. Trim the edge even with the pie plate.
4. For the apple filling: In a mixing bowl, combine all ingredients for the apples. Stir until well mixed. Spoon the mixture into the unbaked pie shell. Use the remaining half of the dough to roll out the top crust, following the same instructions as for the bottom crust. Lift it onto the filled pie and trim 1/2 inch beyond the edge of the pie plate.
5. Fold the top edge under the bottom crust and flute as desired. Cut slits or designs in the top crust to allow steam to escape. Brush with milk and sprinkle with white sugar.
6. Bake at 450°F for 20 minutes. Reduce heat to 350°F and bake an additional 25 to 30 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown. Cool and serve.
